hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 6, 2. Both players called a raise before the cards were dealt.
Turn — 7. Your opponent led out with a bet. You called.
River — 9. Your opponent fired again — half the pot.

wrongReasons: 8 4 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 8 4 would have.
J 4 makes a flush here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
2 A has little reason to lead the river here — high card can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 8 K

Your opponent flopped a straight and immediately raised to build the pot. They led every remaining street — consistent value betting across all streets is a strong signal of a made hand.
